question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 (the AS 65000 Route Reflector) removes the `route-reflector-client`
flag from its R4 neighbor's address-family. The iBGP TCP session stays
Up, but R3 now treats R4 as a normal iBGP peer — R3 only re-advertises
R4-originated routes to its eBGP peers (none in this lab) and other
non-client iBGP peers (none), and crucially **does not** reflect them
to its other RR-clients (R2, R6, R7). Result: R2, R6, R7 stop seeing
R4's eBGP-learned prefixes (192.88.99.11..20) and any redistributed-
connected /30s from R4. Subtle fault — no syslog, only data-plane.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3 (the AS 65000 route reflector), the `route-reflector-client` flag was removed from the R4 iBGP neighbor.
Mechanism: a route reflector only reflects routes learned from a client to its other clients; with R4 no longer marked as a client, R3 treats R4 as a plain iBGP peer and the no-readvertisement-to-iBGP-peers rule applies, so R4-originated routes are not reflected to R3's remaining clients (R2, R6, R7). The iBGP TCP session itself is unaffected, so the failure is purely in the control-plane route distribution: R2, R6, R7 never receive R4's eBGP-learned prefixes (192.88.99.11-20) and redistributed connecteds, leaving those destinations unreachable from them.
Fix: re-add `route-reflector-client` to R3's R4 neighbor address-family.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.4
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  no route-reflector-client

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 65000
neighbor 192.0.2.4
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-reflector-client

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:44:39.965 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Down - RR client configuration changed (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:44:44.984 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:44:39.970 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.3 Down - Peer closing down the session (VRF: default) (AS: 65000)
